name: Garry's Tex-Mex Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 1 large can chili with beef (no beans), your favorite brand
recipeInstructions:
When I was growing up, these enchiladas with rice and refried beans were the essence of Mexican food as I knew it. I now make a variety of more sophisticated enchilada sauces and use different cheeses, but I still make these occasionally when I get the craving for them. These are also often part of family gatherings with my mother and brother.
Warning: This recipe is not low-fat.
1. Heat just enough vegetable oil in a frying pan to cover the bottom over medium flame.
2. Fry the tortillas briefly in the oil until heated through and softened. Avoid overheating or frying for too long, as this will make them hard and crisp, preventing rolling.
3. Place the tortillas on a plate covered with a paper towel.
4. Grate American cheese (not imitation) – Land-O-Lakes white American is recommended.
5. Sprinkle grated cheese and chopped onion onto each tortilla and carefully roll into a tube, placing seam side down in a casserole dish.
6. Pour the can of chili over the filled tortillas evenly.
7. Bake at 350°F until the enchiladas are heated through and the chili is bubbling slightly. Sprinkle any remaining cheese on top and return to the oven until melted.
Serve with Mexican rice and refried beans.
